listOfQuestions.question: In Organic Chemistry, what does "Hydrocarbon" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A compound made only of carbon and hydrogen
wrongAnswers: A hydrocarbon with at least one carbon-carbon double bond
An organic compound containing nitrogen bonded to carbon or hydrogen
A compound with no carbon-carbon multiple bonds

listOfQuestions.question: In Organic Chemistry, what does "Alkane" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A hydrocarbon with only single carbon-carbon bonds
wrongAnswers: A hydrocarbon with at least one carbon-carbon triple bond
A large molecule made from repeating smaller units
A compound with at least one carbon-carbon multiple bond

listOfQuestions.question: In Organic Chemistry, what does "Alkene" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A hydrocarbon with at least one carbon-carbon double bond
wrongAnswers: An organic compound containing a hydroxyl group
A small molecule that can join to form a polymer
A stable ring compound with delocalized electrons

listOfQuestions.question: In Organic Chemistry, what does "Alkyne" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A hydrocarbon with at least one carbon-carbon triple bond
wrongAnswers: An organic compound containing a carboxyl group
A compound with the same formula but a different structure
A substance that speeds a reaction without being consumed

wrongAnswers: An organic compound often formed from an alcohol and a carboxylic acid
A group of atoms that gives a molecule characteristic reactions
A reaction where atoms add across a multiple bond
